div.p: Message structures define the arrangement and ordering of segments within an HL7 v2+ message. They specify which segments are required, optional, or repeating, and how segments are grouped together.
A message structure is the abstract syntax tree (AST) that defines the valid composition of segments for a particular message type. Multiple trigger events may share the same message structure.

The ADT_A01 message structure is used for the Admit/Visit Notification event (A01). It is one of the most widely implemented message structures in HL7 v2 and illustrates the typical pattern of an ADT message:
div.pre: MSH Message Header (Required) ARV Access Restrictions (Optional, Repeating) SFT Software Segment (Optional, Repeating) UAC User Authentication Credential (Optional) EVN Event Type (Required) PID Patient Identification (Required) PD1 Additional Demographics (Optional) OH1-OH4 Occupational Health segments (Optional) ARV Access Restrictions (Optional, Repeating) ROL Role (Deprecated - use PRT) PRT Participation (Optional, Repeating) NK1 Next of Kin (Optional, Repeating) PV1 Patient Visit (Required) PV2 Patient Visit - Additional Info (Optional) ... Additional segment groups

p.i: The full v2+ specification includes 419 message structure definitions. This demo build includes a representative subset.
